mission.title: volcano: scheduling with update from v1.2.0 to v1.4.0
mission.description: scheduling with update from v1.2.0 to v1.4.0. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify volcano troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your volcano deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n volcano -l app.kubernetes.io/name=volcano
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=volcano -n volcano --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review volcano configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant volcano configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n volcano -l app.kubernetes.io/name=volcano
kubectl get configmap -n volcano -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=volcano
```
**What happened**:

volcano update from 1.2.0 to 1.4.0. With the newest version if there are not enough resources `PodGroup`s are kept in `Pending` phase and cluster autoscaler does not trigger to provision more resources.

steps.title: Apply the fix for scheduling with update from v1.2.0 to v1.4.0
steps.description:
#1672 introduced fine-grained Reasons to give users more context about why scheduling did not succeed. However, this breaks compatibility with cluster autoscalers like Karpenter, which rely on the reason being set to `Unschedulable` to trigger scale-up, and the more fine-grained reason `Undetermined` causes the autoscaler to skip the pod.

This PR removes this reason to fix support for cluster autoscalers.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm scheduling with update from v1.2.0 to v1.4.0 is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=volcano -n volcano --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n volcano --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: #1672 introduced fine-grained Reasons to give users more context about why scheduling did not succeed. However, this breaks compatibility with cluster autoscalers like Karpenter, which rely on the reason being set to `Unschedulable` to trigger scale-up, and the more fine-grained reason `Undetermined` causes the autoscaler to skip the pod.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with volcano installed or the issue environment reproducible.
